hero.subheadline: A public changelog, an in-app 'What's New' badge, and automatic emails to subscribers — set up in 5 minutes, not 5 hours.
hero.urgency: ✦ {spotsLeft} of 10 founding spots left · first 10 get 1 month Pro free
hero.mockupEntry1Desc: Install our 'What's New' badge in your app. Users click to see all recent updates.
hero.mockupEntry2Desc: Your changelog lives at yourapp.com/changelog. Beautiful, brandable, fast.
hero.mockupEntry3Desc: Every update sent to subscribers automatically. Zero config required.

problem.title: You ship. They don't notice.
problem.subtitle: Features only retain users when users know they exist.
problem.card1Desc: No update since 2020. You're paying for an abandoned product. Your users deserve better.
problem.card2Desc: Enterprise pricing for an indie problem. That's your entire MRR — for a changelog.
problem.card3Desc: A shared doc with no widget, no emails, no branding. Your users never find it anyway.

solution.title: One tool. Three ways to keep users hooked.
solution.feature1Desc: A dedicated page at yourapp.com/changelog. Chronological, filterable, fully branded. The kind of page that makes your product look professional from day one.
solution.feature2Desc: A badge that lives inside your product. One click — users see every update. Install in 2 minutes. Stop losing users to features they didn't know existed.
solution.feature3Desc: Publish once. Every subscriber gets a clean email automatically. No Mailchimp, no sequences, no extra work.
solution.feature4Desc: Connect your repo and your changelog writes itself. Review the draft, hit publish. From merge to shipped story in seconds.

codeSnippet.title: Works with any stack.
codeSnippet.subtitle: One script tag. No npm install, no framework SDK, no config file.
codeSnippet.b1: Live on your domain in 2 minutes
codeSnippet.b3: Under 4 KB — zero impact on performance
codeSnippet.label: Add to your app

howItWorks.title: Live in 5 minutes. Seriously.
howItWorks.step1Desc: Sign up, name your project. Your public changelog URL is ready immediately — no setup, no config.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Write a clean entry in the editor. Add a tag, a version number, a screenshot. Hit publish.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Your changelog is live. Your widget updates. Subscribers get the email. You're done.

testimonials.title: What our users are saying
testimonials.subtitle: Fake reviews section. Product in development. Click or hover a card — there's a second layer. Still fake though.
testimonials.t1Text: This is a fake review. I don't exist. But if I did, I'd tell you my Notion changelog had zero views this month and that $6.99/month is genuinely reasonable. Sign up and replace me.
testimonials.t2Text: I'm an AI simulating a 5-star review for a landing page. In real life, this spot is yours. Join the waitlist. I'm counting on you to make this section real.
testimonials.t3Text: My name is Placeholder, my review is empty, and yet you're reading this far. If that's not proof that curiosity leads to action, I don't know what is. Sign up.
testimonials.t4Text: This card was generated at 2am. I have no opinion on Noteship because I don't exist. But my creator told me $6.99/month for all this is a solid deal. I trust her.
testimonials.t5Text: This spot is reserved for you. I'm holding it until Noteship ships. The moment you join the waitlist, I disappear. It's in your hands.
testimonials.t6Text: Six fake reviews. Six empty spots to fill. If you're still reading this card without clicking 'Claim your spot', you're testing your own willpower. Spoiler: the button is right below.
testimonials.b1Text: You flipped me. Bold. This is what peak engagement looks like. Still a fake review, product still in dev. But you're now statistically more likely to sign up. Use that energy.
testimonials.b2Text: Welcome to the back of the card. Same product. Same fake review. Different vibe. $6.99/month. Still not $99/month. Still launching this summer. Join the waitlist.
testimonials.b3Text: You found the secret face. Nothing here but another fake testimonial and the quiet realization you've been reading placeholder text for 30 seconds. That's called engagement. Sign up.
testimonials.b4Text: In a parallel universe I'm a real user raving about how Noteship fixed my retention. In this one I'm a CSS card flip. But the $6.99/month is real. The launch is real. The waitlist is open.
testimonials.b5Text: Front me: fake review. Back me (hi): also fake, now with 3D perspective. The irony is this entire section is more memorable than any real testimonial on any competitor site.
testimonials.b6Text: 12 reviews. 0 real users. 100% honest about it. You've read every single one. At this point clicking 'Claim your spot' is the only logical next step. You've earned it.

founderStory.title: Built by a founder tired of shipping in the dark.
founderStory.body: I was shipping features every week and getting nothing back. No reactions, no feedback, no idea if anyone even noticed. My changelog was a Notion doc with zero monthly readers. I spent more time building features than telling people about them. I built Noteship to fix exactly that — a changelog that actually reaches users, without the $99/month price tag.
founderStory.philosophy: We handle the distribution. You write the story.

earlyAccess.title: Get in before the doors open.
earlyAccess.subtitle: We're launching this summer. 10 founding spots — first come, first served. Not just early users: the people who shape the roadmap.
earlyAccess.perk1: 1 month Pro free — no credit card, no conditions
earlyAccess.perk2: Direct line to the builder — not a support ticket
earlyAccess.perk3: Your feedback ships in days, not quarters
earlyAccess.perk4: Founding Member badge on your profile, forever

waitlistForm.success: You're in. Check your inbox for confirmation.
waitlistForm.errorInvalid: Please enter a valid email address.
waitlistForm.errorDuplicate: You're already on the list — we'll reach out soon!

faq.title: Questions.
faq.a1: This summer. Founding members get notified first and unlock 1 month Pro free with no strings attached.
faq.q2: Is there really a free plan?
faq.a2: Yes, forever. One project, up to 10 subscribers, and a full public changelog page — no credit card required. Enough to start and validate.
faq.q3: How does the widget embed work?
faq.a3: One script tag in your HTML. No npm install, no framework-specific SDK, no configuration file. It works with React, Vue, plain HTML — whatever you ship with.
faq.q4: What's different from Headway or Beamer?
faq.a4: Headway hasn't shipped an update since 2020. Beamer charges $99/month — often more than an indie product's entire MRR. Noteship is actively maintained, costs $6.99, and was built for makers, not enterprise teams.
faq.q5: What happens after I join the waitlist?
faq.a5: You get a confirmation email immediately. When we launch this summer, you'll be among the first to get access — with all your founding member perks intact.

pricing.title: Priced for makers, not enterprises.
pricing.freeF2: Up to 10 subscribers — enough to start
